title: Camera control mechanism for an aerial vehicle
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a control system for a retractable belly mounted payload in an Aerial vehicle for retraction, pan and tilt movements of the payload, comprising a first mechanism to rotate the payload about a vertically oriented first axis for pan movement of the payload, and a second mechanism to rotate the payload about a horizontally oriented second axis, wherein rotation of the payload about the second axis provides movement of the payload between a retracted position within belly of the Aerial Vehicle and a deployed position out of the belly of the Aerial Vehicle, as well as tilt movement of the payload in its deployed position. First mechanism is driven by a servo through a set of gears or pulley-belt mechanism, and second mechanism is driven by a directly connected servo connected to the payload. Both the servos rotate along with payload during pan movement.
claims: Claims We Claim: 1. A control system for a retractable belly mounted payload in an Aerial vehicle for retraction, pan and tilt movements of the payload, the system comprising: a first mechanism to rotate the payload about a first axis, wherein the first axis is a vertical axis for the pan movement of the payload; and a second mechanism to rotate the payload about a second axis that is perpendicular to the first axis; wherein the rotation of the payload about the second axis provides movement of the payload between a retracted position within belly of the Aerial Vehicle and a deployed position out of the belly of the Aerial Vehicle; and wherein the rotation of the payload about the second axis also provides the tilt movement of the payload in its deployed position. 2. The control system as claimed in claim 1, wherein the payload is a camera having a line of sight. 3. The control system as claimed in claim 2, wherein the second axis is perpendicular to the line of sight of the camera and is located with an offset from the line of sight. 4. The control system as claimed in claim 3, wherein the offset between the second axis and the line of sight is such that the camera and the line of sight is fully within the belly of the Aerial Vehicle when the second mechanism is in a zero position; and when the second mechanism is rotated by 180 degrees from the zero position, the camera partially projects out of the belly such that the line of sight of the camera is outside the belly. 5. The control system as claimed in claim 4, wherein the line of sight of the camera is horizontally placed when the second mechanism is at 180 degrees from the zero position; and wherein rotation of the second mechanism to positions at less than 180 degrees provides the tilt movements to the camera. 6. A control system for a retractable belly mounted payload in an Aerial vehicle for retraction, pan and tilt movements of the payload, the system comprising: a mounting bracket configured to be fixed to the Aerial Vehicle within the belly; a payload casing configured to rotate relative to the mounting bracket along a first axis; a transmission mechanism comprising a drive member driven by a first servo motor and a driven member; wherein the driven member is fixed to the mounting bracket concentric to the first axis, and the drive member and the first servo motor are mounted on the payload casing and are configured for planetary rotation about the driven member and in the process rotate the casing about the first axis; wherein the first axis is vertically oriented and provides the pan movement to the payload mounted on the payload casing. 7. The control system as claimed in claim 6, wherein the transmission mechanism is a set of gears. 8. The control system as claimed in claim 6, wherein the transmission mechanism is a pulley and belt mechanism. 9. The control system as claimed in claim 6, wherein the payload is pivotally mounted on the payload casing for rotation about a second axis that is perpendicular to the first axis, and rotation of the payload about the second axis provides movement of the payload between a retracted position within belly of the Aerial Vehicle and a deployed position out of the belly of the Aerial Vehicle; and wherein the rotation of the payload about the second axis also provides the tilt movement of the payload in its deployed position. 10. The control system as claimed in claim 6, wherein the control system further comprises a second servo motor mounted on the payload casing and directly coupled to the payload to rotate the payload about the second axis. 11. The control system as claimed in claim 6, wherein the mounting bracket is mounted within the belly of the Aerial Vehicle by suitably positioned hardware. 12. The control system as claimed in claim 6, wherein the mounting bracket and the payload casing incorporate spring loaded contacts to provide electrical connectivity between the Aerial Vehicle and the payload casing for the first servo motor and the second servo motor even as the payload casing rotates about the first axis.
